articles.title: Consumption hit as kitchens feel the heat of LPG shortage
articles.description: India's culinary landscape is feeling the pressure as a lack of LPG supplies disrupts the food industry. The decline in wheat flour sales poses challenges for the production of staples such as bread and biscuits.

articles.description: Vijay is highly likely to make his electoral debut from the Perambur constituency, sources in the party say

articles.title: Election officials seize ₹6 lakh unaccounted cash in Vellore, nearby districts
articles.description: In a similar incident, election officials seized ₹2.5 lakh from textile traders near Ambur town in Tirupattur when they stopped their vehicle on Chennai - Bengaluru Highway (NH 48)

topStories.title: A pharmacist and a homesick lifestyle blogger: The 'alarming' civilian cost of war in Iran
topStories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

topStories.title: 'Like the plane got cut in half': Eyewitnesses describe chaos of LaGuardia crash
topStories.description: Eyewitnesses have described the moment Air Canada flight AC8646 crashed into a fire truck on the runway of New York's LaGuardia airport.

topStories.title: Supreme Court unhappy over CBI, ED ‘reluctance’ against Anil Ambani firms, calls for ‘fair, dispassionate’ investigation
topStories.description: The Supreme Court today called on the CBI and ED to conduct a “fair, dispassionate, transparent, and time-bound” investigation into a banking fraud linked to the Anil Dhirubhai Ambani Group. The apex court also directed concerned financial institutions to “render full cooperation”.

topStories.title: EAM Jaishankar speaks with US' secretary Rubio on West Asia conflict
topStories.description: Jaishankar and Rubio discussed West Asia conflict and its impact on the global economy, with a focus on energy security concerns

topStories.title: JFK, Atlanta airports issue travel advisory amid TSA worker shortage; passengers can expect five-hour wait time
topStories.description: Two of the US' busiest airports issued a travel advisory on Monday, warning passengers that the wait time could be at least five hours as TSA workers resign amid a partial DHS shutdown. Atlanta Airport, the world's busiest, saw wait time stretching beyond three hours over the weekend.

topStories.title: Joe Kent praises Trump, says ‘President working to get peace with Iran, Israel will be against it, but…’
topStories.description: Kent, who is also former counterterrorism official in the Trump administration, said the President was working to make peace with Iran, while once again acknowledging resistance from Israel.

topStories.title: ‘I Messed Up’: Harrowing Final Moments in Fatal LaGuardia Crash
topStories.description: Air-traffic controllers were helping a fire truck’s crew navigate a rain-slicked tarmac at New York’s LaGuardia before midnight on Sunday when panic suddenly set in.

dailyNews.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

dailyNews.description: While ICE agents have been deployed to support basic tasks, travelers continue to face long waits and confusion, especially at busy hubs like Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.

dailyNews.description: A 13-foot Christopher Columbus replica now stands on White House grounds, replacing the monument rioters tossed into Baltimore's Inner Harbor in 2020.

dailyNews.title: Spring break hot spots turn lawless as fights erupt, drugs flow and dozens arrested in sweeping crackdowns
dailyNews.description: Fort Lauderdale and Daytona Beach racked up dozens of spring break arrests this weekend as Florida cities impose curfews and crack down on beach chaos.

dailyNews.title: Critic slams Chicago's 'revolving door' as Loyola student killing sparks outrage
dailyNews.description: Sheridan Gorman's family demands accountability after the Loyola University student's murder, calling it preventable and rejecting the explanation of "wrong place, wrong time."

dailyNews.title: Parkinson's risk increases with exposure to common chemical, study suggests
dailyNews.description: New research from UCLA links prolonged exposure to the pesticide chlorpyrifos to increased Parkinson’s risk, with supporting evidence from both human and animal studies.

dailyNews.description: Newly released oral histories revealed that former DNI Dennis Blair confronted President Obama over Iran’s nuclear policy and was later sidelined.

dailyNews.title: Trump Delays Energy Strikes, but Iran’s Infrastructure is Already Battered
dailyNews.description: U.S. and Israeli attacks on power plants and other civilian infrastructure risk escalating the conflict across the region, and angering Iranians who oppose the government.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration to Pay $1 Billion to Energy Giant to Cancel Wind Farms
dailyNews.description: In exchange, the French company TotalEnergies would invest in oil and natural gas projects in Texas and elsewhere.

dailyNews.title: Trump Is Digging Up Washington. Can Lawsuits Stop the Bulldozers?
dailyNews.description: As the president develops plans to fundamentally alter the White House, the Kennedy Center and other sites, federal lawsuits are beginning to catch up.

dailyNews.title: Trump says Strait of Hormuz deadline delayed amid talks with Iran
dailyNews.description: As Iran threatens to mine the entire Persian Gulf, President Trump says his deadline for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen is postponed amid negotiations with Tehran.

dailyNews.title: Strait of Hormuz dotted with about a dozen Iranian mines, U.S. officials say
dailyNews.description: U.S. officials say assessments show at least a dozen Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz as President Trump backs off his threat to target Iran's energy infrastructure over claims of productive peace talks

dailyNews.title: Iran’s President Hashemi Rafsanjani (1997) | 60 Minutes Archive
dailyNews.description: In 1997, Mike Wallace returned to Iran to interview President Hashemi Rafsanjani and asked whether Iran wanted to build a nuclear bomb.

dailyNews.title: 3/23: Face the Nation
dailyNews.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," National Security Adviser Mike Waltz and Senate Homeland Security chair Rand Paul join as the backlash grows against the Trump administration for the deportation of alleged Venezuelan gang members. Plus. House intelligence committee member Democratic Rep. Jim Himes joins.

dailyNews.title: Dow jumps after Trump pushes back Iran ultimatum
dailyNews.description: The stock market is responding favorably after President Trump said Monday morning that he would postpone his ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Mr. Trump had previously threatened to "obliterate" Iran's power plants if the strait wasn't reopened by Monday evening. Sabrina Escobar, a reporter for Barron's, joins CBS News to discuss the markets.

dailyNews.title: ICE agents spotted in airports across the country in plan to assist TSA
dailyNews.description: ICE agents have been spotted in airports across the nation after President Donald Trump announced they would be deployed to assist TSA. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch reports on the morning rush at Atlanta’s Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport and how ICE agents are helping.

dailyNews.title: Trump says Israel would be 'very happy' if U.S. reaches ceasefire deal with Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump told reporters he believed Israel would abide by a ceasefire agreement negotiated between the United States and Iran. The president also discussed the decision to hold off on strikes on a power plant as talks between the nations were ongoing.

dailyNews.title: Trump rejects off-ramp to fund DHS as airport delays worsen
dailyNews.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune discussed an off-ramp with President Donald Trump to reopen TSA and end long lines at airports, funding DHS but not ICE. Trump rejected it.
